---
title: "About"
---

Hi there! I’m Edward, an undergrad in the class of 2024 and an Egleston Scholar at Columbia Engineering.

My main interests lie amidst the fields of Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing, and Computational Neuroscience, and my lifetime goal is to contribute to the ongoing pursuit of comprehending what exactly governs consciousness and sentience.

Currently, I'm working with Professor Nakul Verma and leading a research project on unsupervised sentiment analysis with finance-related articles. The motivation behind the project is, to a) develop a robust model for classifying whether articles talk about a certain company positively or negatively, and b) to develop a method that correctly classifies unlabeled finance-related news articles.

I'm also collaborating with a friend who goes to Cambridge University on a Computer Vision-related project. The general idea is to develop a miniature system that can accurately calculate object distance by focusing on the "blurriness" of the picture (the Laplacian Values) that uses deep learning.

Besides research, I'm also in Columbia's space development club, also known as Columbia Space Initiative (CSI). I'm in the RASC-AL team that looks to participate in RASC-AL, a student competition to design deep space mission concepts. Specifically, I'm in charge of designing and researching about the propulsion systems.

While I was in Columbia's Formula Racing Club (FSAE), I was working on tire simulation; specifically, applying curve-fitting and coefficient optimization of Pacejka's Magic Formula.

In my free time, I like to program–mainly simulations that I find interesting to see what would happen and reinforcement learning agents to train on the games I make. I also like making robots and tinkering.

If you're interested, here are a few miscellaneous links (most are in Japanese) where I appear:
 - [The Egleston Scholars: Weida (Edward) Li] (https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/egleston-scholars/weida-edward-li)
 - [江副記念財団学術部門第四十九期生：李 為達](https://www.recruit-foundation.org/academic/li/)
 - [Containerインタビュー：李 為達](https://container-web.jp/people/15564.html)
 - [現役高校生チーム、アメリカのロボット大会に臨む！](https://newswitch.jp/p/10882)

Feel free to reach out to me for anything!
 - Email: [wl2787@columbia.edu](mailto:wl2787@columbia.edu)
 - GitHub: [IronEdward](https://github.com/IronEdward)
